title: USA World Cup 2026 Roster: Pochettino's Squad and Christian Pulisic Status
original_text:
The USA's World Cup 2026 squad: Pochettino's 26 and a home tournament
Mauricio Pochettino named the United States' 26-man World Cup 2026 roster on May 26, 2026, in New York, with Christian Pulisic the headline name as the host nation prepares for a home tournament. The squad mixes 13 returnees from the 2022 World Cup with 13 first-timers and ranks as the fifth-youngest the USA has ever sent to a World Cup. Below are all 26 players by position, the USA's Group D fixtures, the Pulisic update, and where to back the Stars and Stripes among our World Cup 2026 betting sites.
Last updated: June 4, 2026. This is the USA's final 26, confirmed ahead of the June 1 FIFA deadline. Tournament rules allow an injured player to be replaced up to 24 hours before the opener against Paraguay on June 12.
(Photo by Jared C. Tilton/Getty Images)
USA World Cup 2026 roster TL;DR
Head coach: Mauricio Pochettino (in charge since September 2024)
Roster status: Final 26-man squad, announced May 26, 2026
Key player: Christian Pulisic (AC Milan), the team's most experienced player
Group: Group D, with Paraguay, Australia and Türkiye
First match: June 12 vs Paraguay, SoFi Stadium, Inglewood (Los Angeles) (9:00 PM ET)
Squad makeup: 3 goalkeepers, 10 defenders, 6 midfielders, 7 forwards
World Cup history: Best finish third (1930), round of 16 in 2022, co-host in 2026, 11th appearance
USA World Cup 2026 roster: full squad list
(Photo by Kevin C. Cox/Getty Images)
Pochettino leaned on a core of European-based players supported by MLS regulars, with Christian Pulisic the standout name. The list groups every player by position, with the club each was attached to at selection.
PositionPlayerClubNote
Goalkeeper Matt Turner New England Revolution
Goalkeeper Matt Freese New York City FC
Goalkeeper Chris Brady Chicago Fire
Defender Sergiño Dest PSV Eindhoven
Defender Antonee Robinson Fulham
Defender Chris Richards Crystal Palace
Defender Tim Ream Charlotte FC
Defender Miles Robinson FC Cincinnati
Defender Mark McKenzie Toulouse
Defender Auston Trusty Celtic
Defender Joe Scally Borussia Mönchengladbach
Defender Max Arfsten Columbus Crew
Defender Alex Freeman Villarreal
Midfielder Tyler Adams Bournemouth
Midfielder Weston McKennie Juventus
Midfielder Malik Tillman Bayer Leverkusen
Midfielder Gio Reyna Borussia Mönchengladbach
Midfielder Cristian Roldan Seattle Sounders
Midfielder Sebastian Berhalter Vancouver Whitecaps
Forward Christian Pulisic AC Milan Most experienced (84 caps)
Forward Folarin Balogun AS Monaco
Forward Ricardo Pepi PSV Eindhoven
Forward Tim Weah Olympique Marseille
Forward Brenden Aaronson Leeds United
Forward Haji Wright Coventry City
Forward Alex Zendejas Club América
Squad total: 26 (3 goalkeepers, 10 defenders, 6 midfielders, 7 forwards).
Is Christian Pulisic in the USA's World Cup 2026 squad?
Yes. Christian Pulisic is in the squad and is the player the USA's hopes rest on. The AC Milan forward is the most experienced name on the roster with 84 caps, the team's leading attacker, and widely seen as its on-field leader, though Tyler Adams and Tim Ream have also worn the captain's armband during this cycle.
Pochettino rested Pulisic during the 2025 Gold Cup to manage his workload, a decision the coach defended as protecting his best player for the World Cup rather than chasing chemistry in the off-season. Pulisic comes into the tournament fit and in form, and he will carry the creative load from the left and through the middle.
Around him, Folarin Balogun and Ricardo Pepi give Pochettino genuine goal threat, while Weston McKennie, Tyler Adams and Malik Tillman form an experienced midfield. Gio Reyna, one of the most naturally gifted players in the pool, made the squad despite limited club minutes, a sign Pochettino values match-winning talent. This section will be updated if Pulisic's status changes before the opener.
Who was left out of the USA's World Cup 2026 roster?
The most talked-about omission was Real Salt Lake midfielder Diego Luna, who had become a regular under Pochettino but missed the final cut. Lyon midfielder Tanner Tessmann was the other notable absence, left out after his club shut him down late in the season with a muscle strain.
Atalanta midfielder Yunus Musah, a starter at the 2022 World Cup, also missed out after a season short of regular minutes. With the squad deep in attack and midfield, Pochettino had several difficult calls to make.
USA World Cup 2026 fixtures
(Photo by Scott Strazzante/Getty Images)
The USA play two of their three group games at SoFi Stadium in the Los Angeles area, with a trip to Seattle in between. As Group D's highest-ranked side, they are favoured to advance.
DateOpponentVenueTime (ET)
Fri, June 12 Paraguay SoFi Stadium, Inglewood (Los Angeles) 9:00 PM
Fri, June 19 Australia Lumen Field, Seattle 3:00 PM
Thu, June 25 Türkiye SoFi Stadium, Inglewood (Los Angeles) 10:00 PM
All times Eastern. For the latest match prices as the group unfolds, our soccer odds page tracks the USA's lines.
USA World Cup 2026 odds comparison
The USA are long shots to win the tournament, even with home advantage, sitting well down the outright board behind the European and South American contenders. Prices vary widely, from around 41.00 to 67.00 in decimal, a reflection of how sportsbooks weigh home advantage against squad depth. The table below compares each sportsbook's price for the USA to lift the trophy. Outright odds move with form, injuries and results, so confirm the live price before betting, and see our World Cup 2026 betting sites guide for the full tournament market.

Best price: USA to win the World Cup 67.00 (BetPlays, 29Black, Coolbet, BetVictor).
Four books share the top price of 67.00, with most of the market clustered around 64.00 to 65.00. The shortest prices are well below that, with Sports Interaction at 41.00, 888sport at 51.00 and 10bet at 60.00, so the spread on the USA is unusually wide. A winning $100 bet returns $6,700 at the top price against $4,100 at the shortest, the biggest shopping gap on any of the contenders we track.
“Odds were captured simultaneously on June 2, 2026 at 8:00 AM ET and move constantly. Confirm the live price at the sportsbook before placing any bet. 19+ (18+ in Alberta, Manitoba and Quebec). Please play responsibly.”
Sol Fayerman-HansenEditor-in-Chief
Editor's take: the USA's World Cup 2026 chances
Home advantage is real, and this is the deepest attacking group the United States has taken to a World Cup. Pulisic is a genuine difference-maker, Balogun and Pepi give them goals, and McKennie, Adams and Tillman can match most midfields. In a wide-open Group D where the USA are the highest-ranked side, anything less than the knockout rounds would be a disappointment.
“How far they go after that is the question. The goalkeeping is unsettled, the back line is young, and one bad night against a top side ends it quickly. I see the USA escaping the group, with a last-16 or quarterfinal run as the realistic ceiling, roared on by home crowds in Los Angeles and Seattle. They are not winning the World Cup at these odds, but as a host with a talented core, they are a live underdog to go deeper than people expect. ”
Sol Fayerman-HansenEditor-in-Chief
Roster status and changes
This is the USA's final 26. Under tournament rules, a player ruled out by a serious injury can be replaced up to 24 hours before the opener, so a late change remains possible if a fitness issue worsens.
Defender Chris Richards made the squad despite a late-season fitness question around Crystal Palace's European final. The USA hosted Senegal in Charlotte on May 31 and close their preparation with a send-off match against Germany in Chicago on June 6, before basing themselves in Irvine, California for the tournament. Any official change will be reflected here, in the player table and in the FAQ.

FAQs on Team USA at World Cup 2026
Common Questions on Team USA at the World Cup 2026
Who is in the USA's World Cup 2026 squad?
Mauricio Pochettino named 26 players: three goalkeepers, 10 defenders, six midfielders and seven forwards. The headline names are Christian Pulisic (AC Milan), Weston McKennie (Juventus), Tyler Adams (Bournemouth) and Folarin Balogun (Monaco). Thirteen of the 26 played at the 2022 World Cup. The full list by position is in the table above.
Is Christian Pulisic playing at the World Cup?
Yes. Pulisic is in the squad and is the USA's most important player, the team's most experienced name with 84 caps and its leading attacker. Pochettino rested him during the 2025 Gold Cup to manage his workload, but he comes into the tournament fit and is expected to start every game from the left or through the middle.
Why was Diego Luna left out of the USA's squad?
Diego Luna had become a regular under Pochettino, but the Real Salt Lake midfielder missed the final 26 in a competitive area of the squad. Pochettino has repeatedly said no place is guaranteed and that he selects for balance and roles, which left several in-form players, including Luna and Tanner Tessmann, out of the group.
Who is the USA's head coach at World Cup 2026?
Mauricio Pochettino, the Argentine coach who took charge in September 2024. He previously managed Tottenham, Paris Saint-Germain and Chelsea. This is his first World Cup leading the United States, and he named the 26-man squad on May 26, 2026, in New York.
When did the USA announce its World Cup 2026 roster?
The USA announced the final 26-man squad on Tuesday, May 26, 2026, at an event in New York City broadcast on FOX. June 1 was the FIFA deadline for final squads, so the roster is official, subject only to the standard injury-replacement window before the first match.
What group is the USA in at World Cup 2026?
The USA is in Group D with Paraguay, Australia and Türkiye, and is the highest-ranked side in the group. The USA play two games in the Los Angeles area and one in Seattle. The top two teams in each group advance to the round of 32, along with the eight best third-placed teams across the 48-nation tournament.
When is the USA's first World Cup 2026 match?
The USA opens against Paraguay on Friday, June 12, 2026 at SoFi Stadium in Inglewood, near Los Angeles, kickoff 9:00 PM ET. They then face Australia on June 19 in Seattle and Türkiye on June 25 back at SoFi Stadium. The USA are co-hosts, along with Canada and Mexico.
